mn33:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn33:10.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* not know the drinking-place?
mn33:10.2: Here, bhikkhus, when the *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*] proclaimed by the Tathāgata is being taught, a *bhikkhu* does not find delight in the meaning, does not find delight in the *dhamma*, does not find gladness connected with the *dhamma*.
mn33:10.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not know the drinking-place.
mn33:11.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* not know the road?
mn33:11.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not understand the noble eightfold path as it truly is.
mn33:11.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not know the road.
mn33:12.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* not skilled in pasture?
mn33:12.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not understand the four *satipaṭṭhānas* as they truly are.
mn33:12.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is not skilled in pasture.
mn33:13.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* drain them dry?
mn33:13.2: Here, bhikkhus, faithful householders invite a *bhikkhu* to accept robe-cloth, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick; there, the *bhikkhu* does not know the proper measure for accepting.
mn33:13.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* drains them dry.
mn33:14.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* not honor with special honor those *bhikkhus* who are elders, of long standing, long gone forth, fathers of the *saṅgha*, leaders of the *saṅgha*?
mn33:14.2: Here, bhikkhus, regarding those *bhikkhus* who are elders, of long standing, long gone forth, fathers of the *saṅgha*, leaders of the *saṅgha*, he does not maintain bodily action of goodwill [*mettaṁ kāyakammaṁ*] toward them, both openly and in private;
mn33:14.3: he does not maintain verbal action of goodwill [*mettaṁ vacīkammaṁ*] toward them, both openly and in private;
mn33:14.4: he does not maintain mental action of goodwill [*mettaṁ manokammaṁ*] toward them, both openly and in private.
mn33:14.5: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not honor with special honor those *bhikkhus* who are elders, of long standing, long gone forth, fathers of the *saṅgha*, leaders of the *saṅgha*.
mn33:14.6: Bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* endowed with these eleven *dhammas* is incapable of attaining growth, increase, and expansion in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*].
mn33:15.1: Bhikkhus, a cowherd endowed with eleven factors is capable of looking after a herd of cattle and making it flourish.
mn33:15.3: Here, bhikkhus, a cowherd knows appearance, is skilled in the marks, is one who removes flies’ eggs, is one who covers over a wound, is one who makes smoke, knows the ford, knows the drinking-place, knows the road, is skilled in pasture, and milks them leaving some over. Those bulls that are fathers of the herd, leaders of the herd, he honors with special honor.
mn33:15.4: Bhikkhus, a cowherd endowed with these eleven factors is capable of looking after a herd of cattle and making it flourish.
mn33:16.1: In the same way, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* endowed with eleven *dhammas* is capable of attaining growth, increase, and expansion in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*].
mn33:16.3: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* knows *rūpa*, is skilled in the marks, is one who removes flies’ eggs, is one who covers over a wound, is one who makes smoke, knows the ford, knows the drinking-place, knows the road, is skilled in pasture, and milks leaving some over. Those *bhikkhus* who are elders, of long standing, long gone forth, fathers of the *saṅgha*, leaders of the *saṅgha* — he honors them with special honor.
mn33:17.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* know *rūpa*?
mn33:17.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* understands as it truly is: whatever *rūpa* there is, all *rūpa* as ‘the four great elements, and *rūpa* derived from the four great elements [*upādāyarūpaṁ*].’
mn33:17.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* knows *rūpa*.
mn33:18.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* skilled in the marks?
mn33:18.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* understands as it truly is: a fool is marked by action, a wise person is marked by action.
mn33:18.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is skilled in the marks.
mn33:19.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* one who removes flies’ eggs?
mn33:19.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not endure an arisen thought [*vitakka*] of sensuality [*kāmavitakkaṁ*]; he abandons it, dispels it, brings it to an end, and makes it cease. An arisen thought [*vitakka*] of ill will [*byāpādavitakkaṁ*] … an arisen thought [*vitakka*] of harming [*vihiṁsāvitakkaṁ*] … He does not endure evil, unskillful *dhammas* whenever they arise; he abandons them, dispels them, brings them to an end, and makes them cease.
mn33:19.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is one who removes flies’ eggs.
mn33:2.1: “Bhikkhus, a cowherd endowed with eleven factors is incapable of looking after a herd of cattle and making it flourish.
mn33:2.3: Here, bhikkhus, a cowherd does not know appearance, is not skilled in the marks, is not one who removes flies’ eggs, is not one who covers over a wound, is not one who makes smoke, does not know the ford, does not know the drinking-place, does not know the road, is not skilled in pasture, and milks them dry. Those bulls that are fathers of the herd, leaders of the herd, he does not honor with special honor.
mn33:2.4: Bhikkhus, a cowherd endowed with these eleven factors is incapable of looking after a herd of cattle and making it flourish.
mn33:20.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* one who covers over a wound?
mn33:20.10: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is one who covers over a wound.
mn33:20.2: Here, bhikkhus, seeing a *rūpa* with the eye, a *bhikkhu* does not seize on the *nimitta* or the details.
mn33:20.3: Since, if he were to dwell with the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriyaṁ*] unguarded, evil unskillful *dhammas* of covetousness and distress might overwhelm him, he practices for its restraint, he guards the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriyaṁ*], he undertakes restraint in the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriye*].
mn33:20.4: Hearing a sound with the ear …
mn33:20.5: Smelling an odor with the nose …
mn33:20.6: Tasting a flavor with the tongue …
mn33:20.7: Touching a tangible with the body …
mn33:20.8: Cognizing a *dhamma* with the mind, he does not seize on the *nimitta* or the details.
mn33:20.9: Since, if he were to dwell with the mind *indriya* [*manindriyaṁ*] unguarded, evil unskillful *dhammas* of covetousness and distress might overwhelm him, he practices for its restraint, he guards the mind *indriya* [*manindriyaṁ*], he undertakes restraint in the mind *indriya* [*manindriye*].
mn33:21.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* one who makes smoke?
mn33:21.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is one who teaches the *dhamma* to others in detail, as he has heard it and as he has learned it.
mn33:21.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is one who makes smoke.
mn33:22.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* know the ford?
mn33:22.2: Here, bhikkhus, there are *bhikkhus* who are learned, versed in the traditions, bearers of *dhamma*, bearers of *vinaya*, bearers of the matrices. A *bhikkhu*, from time to time, approaches them and asks and inquires:
mn33:22.4: What is the meaning of this?’
mn33:22.5: Then those venerables open up what has not been opened up, make plain what has not been made plain, and dispel his doubt about the many *dhammas* that are grounds for doubt.
mn33:22.6: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* knows the ford.
mn33:23.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* know the drinking-place?
mn33:23.2: Here, bhikkhus, when the *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*] proclaimed by the Tathāgata is being taught, a *bhikkhu* finds delight in the meaning, finds delight in the *dhamma*, finds gladness connected with the *dhamma*.
mn33:23.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* knows the drinking-place.
mn33:24.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* know the road?
mn33:24.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* understands the noble eightfold path as it truly is.
mn33:24.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* knows the road.
mn33:25.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* skilled in pasture?
mn33:25.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* understands the four *satipaṭṭhānas* as they truly are.
mn33:25.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is skilled in pasture.
mn33:26.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* milk leaving some over?
mn33:26.2: Here, bhikkhus, faithful householders invite a *bhikkhu* to accept robe-cloth, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
mn33:26.3: There, the *bhikkhu* knows the proper measure for accepting.
mn33:26.4: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* milks leaving some over.
mn33:27.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* honor with special honor those *bhikkhus* who are elders, of long standing, long gone forth, fathers of the *saṅgha*, leaders of the *saṅgha*?
mn33:27.2: Here, bhikkhus, regarding those *bhikkhus* who are elders, of long standing, long gone forth, fathers of the *saṅgha*, leaders of the *saṅgha*, he maintains bodily action of goodwill [*mettaṁ kāyakammaṁ*] toward them, both openly and in private;
mn33:27.3: he maintains verbal action of goodwill [*mettaṁ vacīkammaṁ*] toward them, both openly and in private;
mn33:27.4: he maintains mental action of goodwill [*mettaṁ manokammaṁ*] toward them, both openly and in private.
mn33:27.5: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* honors with special honor those *bhikkhus* who are elders, of long standing, long gone forth, fathers of the *saṅgha*, leaders of the *saṅgha*.
mn33:27.6: Bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* endowed with these eleven *dhammas* is capable of attaining growth, increase, and expansion in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*].”
mn33:27.8: Those *bhikkhus*, satisfied, rejoiced in the *Bhagavā*’s words.
mn33:27.9: The Greater Cowherd is finished, the third.
mn33:3.1: In the same way, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* endowed with eleven *dhammas* is incapable of attaining growth, increase, and expansion in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*].
mn33:3.3: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not know *rūpa*, is not skilled in the marks, is not one who removes flies’ eggs, is not one who covers over a wound, is not one who makes smoke, does not know the ford, does not know the drinking-place, does not know the road, is not skilled in pasture, and drains them dry. Those *bhikkhus* who are elders, of long standing, long gone forth, fathers of the *saṅgha*, leaders of the *saṅgha* — he does not honor them with special honor.
mn33:4.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* not know *rūpa*?
mn33:4.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not understand as it truly is: whatever *rūpa* there is, all *rūpa* as ‘the four great elements, and *rūpa* derived from the four great elements [*upādāyarūpaṁ*].’
mn33:4.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not know *rūpa*.
mn33:5.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* not skilled in the marks?
mn33:5.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not understand as it truly is: ‘A fool is marked by action, a wise person is marked by action.’
mn33:5.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is not skilled in the marks.
mn33:6.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* not one who removes flies’ eggs?
mn33:6.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* endures an arisen thought [*vitakka*] of sensuality [*kāmavitakkaṁ*]; he does not abandon it, dispel it, bring it to an end, or make it cease. An arisen thought [*vitakka*] of ill will [*byāpādavitakkaṁ*] … an arisen thought [*vitakka*] of harming [*vihiṁsāvitakkaṁ*] … He endures evil, unskillful *dhammas* whenever they arise; he does not abandon them, dispel them, bring them to an end, or make them cease.
mn33:6.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is not one who removes flies’ eggs.
mn33:7.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* not one who covers over a wound?
mn33:7.10: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is not one who covers over a wound.
mn33:7.2: Here, bhikkhus, seeing a *rūpa* with the eye, a *bhikkhu* seizes on the *nimitta* and the details.
mn33:7.3: Since, if he were to dwell with the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriyaṁ*] unguarded, evil unskillful *dhammas* of covetousness and distress might overwhelm him, he does not practice for its restraint, he does not guard the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriyaṁ*], he does not undertake restraint in the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriye*].
mn33:7.4: Hearing a sound with the ear …
mn33:7.5: Smelling an odor with the nose …
mn33:7.6: Tasting a flavor with the tongue …
mn33:7.7: Touching a tangible with the body …
mn33:7.8: Cognizing a *dhamma* with the mind, he seizes on the *nimitta* and the details.
mn33:7.9: Since, if he were to dwell with the mind *indriya* [*manindriyaṁ*] unguarded, evil unskillful *dhammas* of covetousness and distress might overwhelm him, he does not practice for its restraint, he does not guard the mind *indriya* [*manindriyaṁ*], he does not undertake restraint in the mind *indriya* [*manindriye*].
mn33:8.1: And how, bhikkhus, is a *bhikkhu* not one who makes smoke?
mn33:8.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is not one who teaches the *dhamma* to others in detail, as he has heard it and as he has learned it.
mn33:8.3: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is not one who makes smoke.
mn33:9.1: And how, bhikkhus, does a *bhikkhu* not know the ford?
mn33:9.2: Here, bhikkhus, there are *bhikkhus* who are learned, versed in the traditions, bearers of *dhamma*, bearers of *vinaya*, bearers of the matrices; but a *bhikkhu*, from time to time, does not approach them to ask and inquire:
mn33:9.4: What is the meaning of this?’
mn33:9.5: Then those venerables do not open up what has not been opened up, do not make plain what has not been made plain, and do not dispel his doubt about the many *dhammas* that are grounds for doubt.
mn33:9.6: That is how, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* does not know the ford.
